# Generated by Claude Toad — Review before use

---
name: cli-patterns
description: Node.js CLI architecture patterns for the claude-toad command-line tool
globs: ["src/**/*.ts", "src/commands/**", "package.json"]
---

> **When to read this skill:** Before editing or creating any command handlers in `src/commands/`, modifying the CLI entry point, adding new CLI flags or arguments, working with user config at `~/.claude-toad/`, or implementing file output to `.claude/` directories. Read this skill completely before adding a new command.

## CLI Architecture — claude-toad Conventions

### Command Structure

claude-toad has four commands. Each is a separate module in `src/commands/` (or a similar structure under `src/`).

```
init       — Scan existing repo → generate .claude/ config
new        — Interactive scaffold → new project with .claude/
package    — Bundle .claude/ → distributable .zip
add-skill  — External sources → skill files via Smidge API
```

### Two-Phase Pipeline

Every command that generates output follows the scan → generate pipeline. These phases must be strictly separated.

```typescript
// Phase 1: SCAN — Pure filesystem, zero AI
// Reads: package.json, tsconfig.json, directory structure, config files
// Returns: ProjectAnalysis (plain data)

// Phase 2: GENERATE — AI-powered via Anthropic API
// Reads: ProjectAnalysis
// Returns: Generated files (CLAUDE.md, skills, agents, commands)

// ✅ Correct separation
async function initCommand(rootDir: string): Promise<void> {
  const analysis = await scanProject(rootDir);       // Phase 1: no API calls
  const config = await generateConfig(analysis);      // Phase 2: API calls here
  await writeOutput(rootDir, config);                 // I/O: write files
}

// ❌ Never mix scanning and API calls
async function initCommand(rootDir: string): Promise<void> {
  const pkg = await readPackageJson(rootDir);
  const framework = await askClaudeAboutFramework(pkg); // NO — scan is pure
}
```

### User Configuration

API keys and user preferences live at `~/.claude-toad/config.json`. Never in the project directory.

```typescript
interface ToadConfig {
  anthropicApiKey?: string;
  smidgeApiKey?: string;
}

// Resolution order for Anthropic API key:
// 1. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable
// 2. ~/.claude-toad/config.json → anthropicApiKey
// 3. Prompt user interactively (first run)

async function resolveApiKey(): Promise<string> {
  const fromEnv = process.env['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'];
  if (fromEnv) return fromEnv;

  const config = await loadUserConfig();
  if (config?.anthropicApiKey) return config.anthropicApiKey;

  throw new ApiKeyError('not-found');
}
```

### Output File Generation

All generated files go into the target project's `.claude/` directory. Write atomically — either all files succeed or none are written.

```typescript
// ✅ Atomic write pattern
async function writeOutput(
  rootDir: string,
  config: GeneratedConfig
): Promise<void> {
  const claudeDir = join(rootDir, '.claude');
  const tempDir = join(rootDir, '.claude-toad-tmp');

  try {
    await mkdir(tempDir, { recursive: true });

    // Write all files to temp directory first
    await writeFile(join(tempDir, 'CLAUDE.md'), config.claudeMd);
    for (const skill of config.skills) {
      await writeFile(join(tempDir, 'skills', skill.filename), skill.content);
    }

    // Atomic rename
    await rename(tempDir, claudeDir);
  } catch (error) {
    await rm(tempDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
    throw error;
  }
}
```

### Console Output

Use structured output. No `console.log` littered throughout. Centralize user-facing messages.

```typescript
// ✅ Centralized output
interface Logger {
  info(message: string): void;
  success(message: string): void;
  warn(message: string): void;
  error(message: string): void;
  step(current: number, total: number, message: string): void;
}

// Usage in commands:
logger.step(1, 3, 'Scanning project...');
const analysis = await scanProject(rootDir);

logger.step(2, 3, 'Generating configuration...');
const config = await generateConfig(analysis);

logger.step(3, 3, 'Writing files...');
await writeOutput(rootDir, config);

logger.success('Done! Generated .claude/ directory');
```

### Exit Codes

Follow Unix conventions. Non-zero exit on failure.

```typescript
// ✅ Proper exit codes
const EXIT_SUCCESS = 0;
const EXIT_GENERAL_ERROR = 1;
const EXIT_MISSING_API_KEY = 2;
const EXIT_SCAN_FAILED = 3;
const EXIT_GENERATION_FAILED = 4;

process.exit(EXIT_SUCCESS);
```

### External API Calls

All API communication (Anthropic, Smidge) is isolated behind client modules. Never call `fetch` directly from command handlers.

```typescript
// ✅ API client abstraction
// src/api-client.ts
async function callAnthropic(
  prompt: string,
  apiKey: string
): Promise<AnthropicResponse> {
  // All fetch logic here
}

// ✅ Smidge client for add-skill command
// src/smidge-client.ts
async function generateSkillFromSource(
  source: SkillSource,
  apiKey: string
): Promise<SkillContent> {
  // All Smidge API logic here
}

// ❌ Never in command handlers
async function initCommand(): Promise<void> {
  const response = await fetch('https://api.anthropic.com/...'); // NO
}
```

### File Path Handling

Always use `node:path` for cross-platform compatibility. Never string-concatenate paths.

```typescript
// ✅ Correct
import { join, resolve } from 'node:path';
const configPath = join(homedir(), '.claude-toad', 'config.json');
const claudeDir = join(rootDir, '.claude');

// ❌ Never concatenate
const configPath = homedir() + '/.claude-toad/config.json';
```

### Input Validation

Validate all user inputs and file contents at the boundary, before passing into business logic.

```typescript
// ✅ Validate at the entry point
async function initCommand(args: string[]): Promise<void> {
  const rootDir = resolve(args[0] ?? '.');

  // Validate it's actually a project
  const hasPackageJson = await fileExists(join(rootDir, 'package.json'));
  if (!hasPackageJson) {
    throw new ScanError('Not a project directory — no package.json found', rootDir);
  }

  // Now safe to proceed
  const analysis = await scanProject(rootDir);
  // ...
}
```

### Generated CLAUDE.md Constraint

Anthropic recommends CLAUDE.md files under 150 lines. This is a hard constraint for the generation phase.

```typescript
// ✅ Enforce the limit
function validateGeneratedClaudeMd(content: string): void {
  const lineCount = content.split('\n').length;
  if (lineCount > 150) {
    throw new Error(
      `Generated CLAUDE.md is ${lineCount} lines — must be under 150`
    );
  }
}
```

### The `package` Command — Zip Format

The package command produces a `.zip` file. Not an npm package, not a tarball.

```typescript
// Output: my-project-claude-config.zip
// Contains the entire .claude/ directory structure
// Installable via: npx claude-toad install <path-to-zip>
```

### The `add-skill` Command — Source Types

The add-skill command accepts multiple source types. Each needs specific handling before sending to Smidge API.

```typescript
type SkillSource =
  | { type: 'youtube'; url: string }
  | { type: 'pdf'; filePath: string }
  | { type: 'url'; url: string }
  | { type: 'audio'; filePath: string }
  | { type: 'video'; filePath: string }
  | { type: 'text'; content: string };
```
